findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted as inline content between page-equivalent sections throughout the HTML document, disrupting reading order.
findings.evidence: PDF has header (officials' names: Pat McCrory/Governor, Donald R. Van Der Vaart/Secretary, Tracy Davis/Director) and footer (State of NC address/phone) as repeating page-level elements. The HTML converts these into inline content, inserting footer text, a colored decorative bar, and the officials' names block between the logo/header area and the MEMORANDUM heading, and again between subsequent content sections (e.g., between the outline and Section I, between later sections). A reader encounters these officials' names and footer text multiple times mid-document.

findings.description: A full-page-width image (612pt wide) is inserted between the Table of Contents and Section I, likely a scan of PDF page 2. All the text content from that page is also rendered as HTML text below the image, creating content duplication.
findings.evidence:
HTML: appears immediately before

I. History/Background

, while the same Section I content is fully rendered as HTML text.

findings.description: The second tag (between the TOC and Section I) has an empty alt attribute (alt=""), providing no descriptive information. If this is a substantive image (e.g., a full-page scan), empty alt text is inadequate; if decorative, it should not be present at all.
